@@ -0,0 +1,48 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9980'
+project: ai-panel
+title: 'AI panel: model picker not keyboard-focusable, and no drill-out from the composer editor'
+---
+## What
+
+Two follow-on keyboard-focus gaps in the AI composer, both the same class as the CM6 drill-in fix (task `01KS0WMB0MT0GVWMHF7BREGKWS`): the spatial-nav leaf is registered but does not actually hand the keyboard to the control.
+
+### 1. Model picker is not keyboard-focusable
+
+Jumping / navigating to the `ui:ai-panel.model-selector` leaf does not let the user keyboard the model picker. `ComposerModelSelect` (`apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.tsx`) wraps the Radix `PromptInputSelectTrigger` in `<AiPanelPressable … onPress={() => {}}>` — the `onPress` is a deliberate **no-op** ("Radix's own trigger handler opens the menu"). But the spatial-nav leaf being focused does **not** put DOM focus on the trigger `<button>`, and Enter on the leaf runs the no-op `onPress`. So a Radix `Select` — which needs DOM focus on its trigger for Space/Enter/↑↓ to open and navigate the listbox — is never actually reachable by keyboard.
+
+Fix: when the model-picker leaf is activated (drill-in / Enter), put real DOM focus on the trigger button (or open the select) so the Radix keyboard interaction works. The `onPress` no-op must become a real focus hand-off — give `ComposerModelSelect` a ref to the `PromptInputSelectTrigger` and have `onPress` call `.focus()` on it (mirroring how the CM6 drill-in command calls `editorRef.current?.focus()`).
+
+### 2. No drill-out from the CM6 editor — Escape doesn't release focus
+
+Once the CM6 prompt has DOM focus, pressing Escape does **not** drill out: focus stays trapped in the contenteditable, so the user cannot press `s` to re-open the jump overlay. The composer's CM6 (`AiPromptComposer`) deliberately wired no Escape binding (its docstring kept Escape as a vim insert→normal toggle and noted "there is no cancel callback").
+
+The established drill-out pattern is `FilterEditorDrillOutWiring` in `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/perspective-tab-bar.tsx`: the CM6 Escape keymap calls an `onClose`/escape callback that (1) `document.activeElement.blur()`s the contenteditable so the caret stops, and (2) dispatches `nav.focus` with the editor scope's FQM to return kernel spatial focus to the scope. After that, the panel is no longer trapping keys and `s` (jump) works again.
+
+Fix: wire the composer's CM6 Escape to drill out — blur the editor and `nav.focus` back to the `ui:ai-panel.composer` scope FQM. Follow `FilterEditorDrillOutWiring`'s structure (an escape callback provided via context or prop, consumed by a CM6 Escape keymap extension). Update the `AiPromptComposer` file docstring — the prior "Escape stays a vim insert→normal toggle, no drill-out" rationale is superseded; Escape now drills out, consistent with the filter formula bar.
+
+## Approach
+
+- **Model picker (`ai-prompt-composer.tsx`):** add a `ref` on `PromptInputSelectTrigger`; `ComposerModelSelect`'s `AiPanelPressable.onPress` calls `triggerRef.current?.focus()` instead of being a no-op. Verify pointer click still opens the select (Radix's own handler) — `onPress` focus must not double-fire or fight Radix.
+- **Drill-out (`ai-prompt-composer.tsx`, possibly a small context like `FilterEditorEscapeContext`):** add an Escape keymap extension to the composer's CM6 `extensions` (a `Prec.highest` `keymap` of `{ key: "Escape", run }`) whose `run` invokes a drill-out callback: `blur()` the active element + dispatch `nav.focus({ args: { fq: <composer scope FQM> } })`. The composer scope FQM is available where the `ui:ai-panel.composer` `AiPanelFocusScope` is mounted — thread the escape callback down (context or prop) the way `FilterFormulaBar` consumes `FilterEditorEscapeContext`. In the no-spatial-stack unit-test path the callback is absent and Escape is an inert CM6 no-op (same as the filter editor).
+- Do not change the Enter-submit / Shift-Enter keymap or the submit/stop control.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Navigating / jumping to the model picker leaf puts DOM focus on the model-select trigger; Space/Enter/↑↓ then open and navigate the model listbox.
+- [ ] Activating the model picker by keyboard selects a model (still calls `onSelectModel`); pointer click still opens it.
+- [ ] Pressing Escape while the CM6 prompt has focus drills out: the editor loses DOM focus and kernel spatial focus returns to the `ui:ai-panel.composer` scope.
+- [ ] After that Escape, pressing `s` re-opens the jump overlay (focus is no longer trapped in the editor).
+- [ ] The Enter-submit / Shift-Enter keymap and the submit/stop control are unchanged.
+- [ ] The `AiPromptComposer` docstring is updated — Escape now drills out (the prior vim-toggle rationale removed).
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] In `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx`: a test that activating the model-picker leaf puts `document.activeElement` on the select trigger.
+- [ ] A test that firing Escape while the CM6 prompt has DOM focus blurs the editor and the kernel focus is the `ui:ai-panel.composer` scope (then a subsequent jump-key works / the editor no longer has focus).
+- [ ] Confirm existing `ai-panel.test.tsx` / `ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx` tests (model selection, submit/stop, Enter keymap) still pass.
+- [ ] Run `cd apps/kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run src/components/ai-panel.test.tsx src/components/ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx src/components/ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx src/components/ai-panel-container.test.tsx` — all green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing model-picker-focus and Escape-drill-out tests first. Study `FilterEditorDrillOutWiring` / `FilterFormulaBarFocusable` in `perspective-tab-bar.tsx` and `filter-editor.tsx` before writing code.

+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-19 16:05)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.tsx:233-253` — `buildEscapeDrillOutExtension` wires the Escape drill-out as a single `Prec.highest` `keymap.of({ key: "Escape", run })` binding for *all* keymap modes, and the function docstring (lines 219-231) plus the file docstring assert it "is intercepted ahead of the vim keymap — Escape drills out of the composer from insert mode too". The codebase's own shared helper `cm-submit-cancel.ts` deliberately does NOT use a keymap binding for the vim path: `buildVimEscapeExtension` (lines 156-188) uses a two-phase DOM `keydown` capture listener precisely because a `keymap.of` Escape binding cannot reliably preempt `@replit/codemirror-vim`'s insert-mode Escape (the insert→normal toggle). In vim insert mode the vim plugin will most likely consume Escape first, so the drill-out will not fire on the first Escape — contradicting the documented behavior. The new Escape test in `ai-panel.spatial.test.tsx:802` runs only under `appShellInvokeImpl`'s `keymap_mode: "cua"`, so the vim insert-mode path is entirely unverified. Either (a) route the vim-mode Escape through the same DOM-capture mechanism the shared helper established (and add a vim-mode Escape drill-out test mirroring `ai-prompt-composer.test.tsx`'s vim keymap test), or (b) if the keymap binding genuinely is sufficient here, prove it with a vim-mode test and correct the shared-helper precedent — and either way soften the docstring claim until it is backed by a test.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/ai-prompt-composer.tsx:344-397` — `ComposerEditorDrillOutWiring`'s docstring says it "mirrors `FilterEditorDrillOutWiring`", but it diverges from that reference in two ways without noting it: the filter version is guarded by an outer `FilterFormulaBarFocusable` that checks `layerKey`/`actions` and then uses the strict `useFullyQualifiedMoniker()` inside the scope, whereas the composer version relies solely on `useOptionalFullyQualifiedMoniker()` returning `null` for the no-spatial-stack fallback. The composer's approach is functionally correct and arguably cleaner (one hook, no outer guard), but the "mirrors" claim is slightly inaccurate — either note the intentional simplification in the docstring or drop the verbatim "mirrors" wording.
\ No newline at end of file
